skill.name: tamagui-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Tamagui projects with config v4 setup, compiler optimization, design tokens, animations, forms, dialogs, sheets, and overlays.
skill.summary: Apply practical Tamagui patterns for performant, accessible cross-platform interfaces.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a high volume of command, network, environment, crypto, reconnaissance, and sensitive-file patterns. Manual review found these are markdown examples, Tamagui documentation links, NODE_ENV build checks, and keyword-only false positives; no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Static external-command matches are markdown examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many Ruby backtick and external command detections occur inside markdown code fences or inline Tamagui examples. They are documentation snippets, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown code blocks that demonstrate Tamagui, React, or build commands. No executable script file or dynamic command runner was found in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Documentation fetch commands require user-initiated network access
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE but low risk: The skill recommends curl commands to retrieve current Tamagui documentation from tamagui.dev. This is outbound network access, but the targets are visible documentation URLs and no secrets or local data are sent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands and domains are explicit in the file. The commands only fetch public documentation pages from tamagui.dev and do not include credential material.

low_findings.title: Environment access is limited to build-mode examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The process.env detections read NODE_ENV to enable or disable Tamagui extraction in documented webpack, Next.js, Babel, and config examples. No secret variable names or exfiltration paths were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed environment access is process.env.NODE_ENV inside a build configuration example. There is no evidence of reading API keys, tokens, database URLs, or environment files.

low_findings.title: Keyword-only blocker detections are false positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Weak crypto, reconnaissance, and Windows SAM detections appear to be triggered by ordinary words such as same, Tamagui, design text, table entries, or animation terminology. No cryptography, credential database access, or system reconnaissance commands were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain headings, tables, UI guidance, or words like same and Tamagui. They do not contain cryptographic functions, credential database paths, or reconnaissance instructions.

content.value_statement: Tamagui projects often lose performance, accessibility, and consistency when patterns are improvised. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical rules for config, components, tokens, and compiler-friendly UI.
actual_capabilities: Explains Tamagui config v4 setup with createTamagui and custom theme patterns.
Provides patterns for Dialog, Sheet, Popover, Tooltip, Select, and Adapt usage.
Guides form structure with Form.Trigger, labels, validation libraries, and accessible inputs.
Documents animation rules for enterStyle, exitStyle, animateOnly, and platform drivers.
Shows compiler optimization guidance for static props, variants, build plugins, and CLI use.
Covers design tokens, fonts, semantic themes, spacing, and cross-platform visual decisions.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run Tamagui builds by itself.
It focuses on Tamagui v1.x, so current APIs may need documentation checks.
It includes example shell commands that users must review before running.
It does not replace manual accessibility testing on web, iOS, and Android.

use_cases.title: Set up a new Tamagui app
use_cases.description: Use the config v4, token, theme, and compiler guidance to start a cross-platform app with consistent defaults.

use_cases.title: Fix complex UI components
use_cases.description: Apply the dialog, sheet, overlay, select, form, and animation rules when component behavior breaks across devices.

use_cases.title: Improve performance and polish
use_cases.description: Use compiler, semantic token, font, and animation patterns to reduce runtime cost and improve visual consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Tamagui component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Tamagui component for accessibility, token use, and common pattern mistakes. Suggest small changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a responsive form
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Tamagui form using Form.Trigger, matching Label and Input identifiers, validation guidance, and accessible error states.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor overlays for touch devices
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor these Dialog, Sheet, Popover, Tooltip, or Select components to use Adapt correctly across web and touch devices.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Tamagui build
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Tamagui setup for compiler extraction, static props, variants, importsWhitelist usage, and production build performance.

output_examples.input: Review my Tamagui Dialog component before release.
output: A checklist of required Title and Description usage.
Notes about Adapt behavior for touch devices.
Concrete fixes for overlay animation and dismissal behavior.

output_examples.input: Help improve a slow Tamagui screen.
output: A summary of dynamic style patterns that block extraction.
Suggestions to replace runtime calculations with variants.
Build configuration checks for compiler optimization.

output_examples.input: Create a reusable design system pattern.
output: Guidance for semantic tokens and theme palettes.
Font and spacing recommendations for cross-platform use.
Component structure advice that stays consistent across web and native.

best_practices: Load the relevant pattern file before changing a related Tamagui component.
Prefer static tokens, variants, and compiler-friendly props for production screens.
Test dialogs, sheets, forms, and overlays with keyboard, touch, and screen reader flows.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on Platform.OS branches when Adapt can express the cross-platform behavior.
Do not omit Dialog titles, descriptions, matching labels, or accessible trigger semantics.
Do not use dynamic inline style calculations when a variant or token can express the state.

faq.question: What Tamagui version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Tamagui v1.x patterns, with emphasis on config v4 and compiler optimization.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains documented example commands that a user or agent may choose to run after review.

faq.question: Can it help with React Native and web together?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance focuses on Tamagui patterns that work across web, iOS, and Android.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers dialog titles, descriptions, labels, triggers, overlays, and touch adaptations.

faq.question: Will it fetch the latest Tamagui documentation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It lists public documentation URLs and commands that can be used when current API details are needed.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend engineers, product engineers, and design system maintainers working with Tamagui interfaces.
